name: option-provider
summary: Typed accessor service for an existing WordPress option.
description: Scaffolds a service class that wraps get_option/update_option/delete_option for a single option key, registered as a singleton in DI.
problem: An option already exists (registered via phpnomad/option, or just used by WordPress) and you want consumers to access it through a typed service rather than sprinkling get_option() calls and string keys across the codebase.
appliesWhen: An option already exists or will be registered separately
You want a single class that owns read/write of that option
Consumers should depend on this service via DI
You expect to add domain methods (e.g. isFeatureEnabled()) over time
avoidWhen: You want both registration and the accessor — use phpnomad/setting
You only need a one-off get_option call — just call it
The option has many sub-fields each needing their own accessor — model it as multiple options or a typed config object
examples: Wrap acme_settings with a typed AcmeSettingsProvider
Add a service for reading the feature_flags option
tradeoffs: One service class plus a DI binding. The class is concrete (no interface) by default — extract an interface if you need to mock it or want multiple backends.
outputs: A {name} service class at lib/Options/{name}.php (autowired via the DI container, no explicit binding required)
postApply: Add typed methods on top of get/set as your usage solidifies
Update defaultValue() to match the option's expected shape
Inject {name} where consumers currently call get_option()

name.aiHint: PascalCase. Suffix with Provider to make the role obvious.

optionName.description: WordPress option key this provider wraps
